sourcePolicy.copyright: Do not scrape copyrighted recipe blogs directly.
allowedSources: AI-assisted original recipes reviewed by the owner
Public-domain or open-license recipes with attribution
Free/public recipe APIs such as TheMealDB, Spoonacular, Edamam, DummyJSON, and Open Food Facts

recipes.title: Orange Chicken
recipes.description: Crispy chicken bites tossed in a sweet orange glaze that kids can help mix.
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb chicken breast, cut into bite-size pieces
Green onion or sesame seeds, optional
instructions: Pat chicken dry, then season with salt and pepper.
Toss chicken pieces with cornstarch until lightly coated on every side.
Pan-cook chicken in a thin layer of oil until golden and cooked through, working in batches if needed.
Simmer orange juice, soy sauce, honey, garlic, and ginger until glossy and slightly reduced.
Toss chicken in the sauce until every piece is coated.
Serve over warm rice and finish with green onion or sesame seeds.
tips: Dry chicken browns better than wet chicken.
Keep the pan from crowding so the coating gets crisp instead of steaming.
For a spicier version, add chili flakes or a spoon of chili garlic sauce to the glaze.
recipes.makeAhead: Cut the chicken and mix the sauce up to one day ahead. Cook the chicken fresh so the coating stays crisp.
servingIdeas: Make orange chicken bowls with cucumber, carrots, and green onion.
recipes.chefNotes: A Shay's Kitchen favorite because it feels fun, familiar, and easy to build confidence with.

recipes.title: Crab Rangoon
recipes.description: Crispy wontons filled with creamy crab, scallions, and a little sweetness.
ingredients: 1 cup crab meat or imitation crab, chopped
Small bowl of water, for sealing
instructions: Stir cream cheese, crab, green onion, garlic powder, Worcestershire, and sugar until evenly mixed.
Place one spoonful of filling in the center of each wonton wrapper.
Wet the wrapper edges with water and pinch closed tightly so the filling stays inside.
Fry, bake, or air-fry until the wrappers are crisp and golden.
Let cool for a minute, then serve with sweet chili sauce.
equipment: Air fryer, oven, or heavy pot for frying
tips: Do not overfill the wrappers or they may split.
Keep unused wrappers covered with a towel so they do not dry out.
If air-frying, lightly spray the outside for better browning.
recipes.makeAhead: Mix the filling a day ahead. Assemble right before cooking, or freeze assembled rangoons in a single layer.
servingIdeas: Pair with fried rice or orange chicken.
Add to a party board with dipping sauces.
recipes.chefNotes: Great for game nights, grazing tables, and a starter that feels special without being fussy.

recipes.title: Yakamein
recipes.description: A New Orleans-style beef noodle bowl with broth, egg, green onion, and cozy spice.
ingredients: 1 lb beef chuck or stew meat
instructions: Season beef with Creole seasoning and garlic powder.
Simmer beef in broth with soy sauce until tender and easy to pull apart.
Boil spaghetti separately so the noodles stay springy.
Taste broth and adjust with hot sauce, salt, or lemon.
Build bowls with noodles, broth, beef, boiled egg, and green onion.
Serve hot with extra hot sauce on the side.
tips: Cook noodles separately so leftovers do not soak up all the broth.
A splash of lemon or pickle juice can brighten the bowl.
Use leftover roast beef when you need a faster version.
recipes.makeAhead: Broth and beef can be made two days ahead. Store noodles separately and assemble bowls when ready to eat.
servingIdeas: Serve as a late-night comfort bowl.
Add extra green onion and hot sauce for a New Orleans-style finish.
Pair with simple greens or a small side salad.
recipes.chefNotes: A personal New Orleans-style comfort bowl that bridges Southern roots and global noodle-bowl energy.

recipes.title: Shrimp and Grits with Green Beans
recipes.description: Creamy grits, juicy shrimp, peppers, and green beans for the kind of plate that feels cooked with care.
instructions: Cook grits low and slow with stock.
Saute peppers, garlic, and green beans.
Spoon shrimp and vegetables over creamy grits.
recipes.chefNotes: This one should feel like a real plate from home, not a generic shrimp-and-grits card.

recipes.title: Braised Oxtails
recipes.description: Tender oxtails braised with aromatics until the gravy turns deep, glossy, and rich.
instructions: Cook aromatics in the same pot.
Braise covered until tender and reduce the gravy.
recipes.chefNotes: A slow-cook confidence recipe for people ready to build patience and flavor.

recipes.title: Cajun Cream Salmon Rotini Pasta
recipes.description: A creamy, lightly spicy rotini pasta with salmon, Cajun seasoning, and a sauce that comes together fast.
instructions: Toss pasta with sauce and flaked salmon.
recipes.chefNotes: Fast enough for a weeknight, polished enough for a date-night plate.

recipes.title: Roasted Broccoli, Zucchini, Red Onion, Cherry Tomatoes, and Sweet Potatoes
recipes.description: A colorful sheet-pan vegetable tray from Shay's real kitchen rotation.
instructions: Roast sweet potatoes first, then add tender vegetables.
Finish when browned at the edges.
recipes.chefNotes: A practical side that can become a bowl, plate, or meal-prep base.

recipes.title: White Chicken Chili / Southwest Chicken Soup
recipes.description: A cozy planned soup with chicken, beans, corn, green chiles, and southwest flavor.
instructions: Simmer chicken with broth, beans, corn, and chiles.
Stir in cream cheese until creamy.
recipes.chefNotes: A flexible soup that can stretch across lunches and busy evenings.

recipes.title: General Tso-Style Chicken
recipes.description: Sweet, tangy, lightly spicy chicken bites inspired by takeout but made for a home kitchen.
instructions: Toss chicken in sauce and serve with rice.
recipes.chefNotes: A home version that keeps the joy of takeout while teaching sauce balance.

recipes.title: Cashew Chicken
recipes.description: A planned weeknight stir-fry with tender chicken, cashews, vegetables, and a savory sauce.
instructions: Finish with cashews and serve over rice.
recipes.chefNotes: A strong candidate for the What Can I Cook flow because it uses common pantry sauces.

recipes.title: Fried Rice
recipes.description: A practical leftover-rice meal that teaches timing, high heat, and seasoning.
instructions: Add cold rice and soy sauce.
Fold eggs back in and finish with green onion.
recipes.chefNotes: A cornerstone leftover recipe for building confidence with what is already in the fridge.

recipes.title: Chicken Parmesan
recipes.description: Crisp breaded chicken layered with marinara and cheese for a family-dinner favorite.
instructions: Bread chicken with flour, egg, and breadcrumbs.
Bake until bubbly and cooked through.
recipes.chefNotes: A classic confidence recipe because it teaches breading, sauce, and oven finishing.

recipes.title: Deviled Eggs
recipes.description: A party-table classic that teaches boiling, peeling, mixing, and seasoning.
instructions: Mix yolks with mayo, mustard, relish, and salt.
Pipe or spoon filling back into whites.
recipes.chefNotes: Small, teachable, and perfect for kid chefs or beginner hosts.

recipes.title: Rotel Dip
recipes.description: A familiar party dip for the Brent & Co. table: creamy, easy, and made for gathering.
instructions: Keep warm and serve with chips.
recipes.chefNotes: The kind of recipe that makes a gathering feel easy.

recipes.title: Party Meatballs
recipes.description: Saucy bite-size meatballs for hosting, potlucks, and game nights.
recipes.chefNotes: A hosting helper for when the table needs something warm and low-stress.

recipes.title: Charcuterie Boards
recipes.description: A no-stress hosting board with cheese, fruit, crackers, dips, and easy visual balance.
instructions: Fill gaps with fruit and nuts.
recipes.chefNotes: A visual confidence recipe for hosting without cooking.

recipes.title: Dessert Charcuterie Board
recipes.description: A sweet board idea with fruit, cookies, chocolate, dips, and pretty little bites.
instructions: Fill gaps so the board feels abundant.
recipes.chefNotes: A playful hosting recipe that kids can help build.

recipes.title: Bourbon Praline Bread Pudding
recipes.description: Custardy bread pudding with pecans and a buttery praline-style sauce.
instructions: Spoon warm praline sauce over the top.
recipes.chefNotes: A Brent & Co. signature dessert candidate.

recipes.title: Blackened Fish
recipes.description: A bold skillet fish idea with blackened seasoning and a fast weeknight feel.
instructions: Finish with lemon and serve right away.
recipes.chefNotes: A technique recipe for learning heat control.

recipes.title: Tuscan Chicken
recipes.description: Creamy chicken with spinach, tomatoes, garlic, and parmesan for a polished dinner.
instructions: Build cream sauce with garlic and tomatoes.
Return chicken and simmer until done.
recipes.chefNotes: A cozy restaurant-at-home dinner idea.

recipes.title: Garlic Wings
recipes.description: Crispy wings tossed in buttery garlic flavor for parties, dinner, or a snack table.
instructions: Season and bake or air-fry wings.
recipes.chefNotes: A strong game-night or weekend platter.

recipes.title: Pineapple Fried Rice
recipes.description: A bright fried rice idea with pineapple, vegetables, egg, and a sweet-savory finish.
instructions: Fold eggs back in and serve.
recipes.chefNotes: A sunny pantry recipe with personality.

recipes.title: Lamb Bone Broth / Soup Idea
recipes.description: A planned soup idea built from lamb bones, aromatics, herbs, and slow simmered broth.
instructions: Roast or brown bones for flavor.
Build soup with vegetables, meat, or noodles.
recipes.chefNotes: Keep this as an idea card until the exact final method is tested.

apiAdapters.name: TheMealDB
apiAdapters.notes: Use API response as inspiration or linked external source; do not copy copyrighted blog text.

apiAdapters.name: DummyJSON Recipes
apiAdapters.notes: Useful for test data while original Brent & Co. recipes are being written.

apiAdapters.name: Open Food Facts
apiAdapters.notes: Useful later for product details and pantry matching.
